skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
stem: Rectangle P has length 24 and width w. Rectangle Q has length 18 and the same length-to-width ratio as rectangle P. What is the width of rectangle Q in terms of w?
rationale: Choice B is correct. The length-to-width ratio of rectangle P is 24:w. If rectangle Q has the same ratio with length 18 and width W, then 24/w = 18/W. Solving for W gives W = 18w/24 = 3w/4. Choice A is incorrect because 4w/3 results from inverting the ratio. Choices C and D are incorrect because they treat the relationship as additive rather than multiplicative.
